text_fr: 37/160. Assistance  au  Lesotho
text_en: 37/160. Assistance to Lesotho

text_fr: L'Assemh/ee generale,
text_en: The Assemh/ee Generale,

text_fr: Rappe/ant la  resolution  402  ( 1976)  du  Conseil  de securite, en date du  22  decembre  1976,  dans laquelle le  Conseil  s'etait notamment  declare  preoccupe  par la situation critique resultant de la fermeture par I' Afri› que du  Sud de certains postes frontieres entre I' Afri› que du Sud et le  Lesotho en vue de forcer le  Lesotho a reconnaitre le  bantoustan du  Transkei,
text_en: Reference to Security Council Resolution 402 (1976) of 22 December 1976, in which the Council declared itself concerned in particular by the critical situation resulting from the closure by Afri of certain border posts between Afri and Lesotho in order to force Lesotho to recognize the Bantustan of Transkei,

text_fr: Felicitant le  Gouvernement du  Lesotho de  sa deci› sion de ne pas reconnaitre le  Transkei, conformement aux  decisions  de  !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies, en  particulier a la resolution  31 /6  A  de  I' Assemblee generale, en date du 26  octobre  1976,
text_en: Commending the Government of Lesotho for its decision not to recognize the Transkei, in accordance with the decisions of the United Nations, in particular resolution 31/6 A of the General Assembly of 26 October 1976,

text_fr: Pleinement consciente que  la  decision  prise  par le Gouvernement du  Lesotho de  ne  pas  reconnaitre  le Transkei a impose a son  peuple  un  fardeau  economi› que special,
text_en: Fully aware that the decision of the Government of Lesotho not to recognize the Transkei has placed on its people an economi› burden that special,

text_fr: Approuvant vigoureusement les  appels que le  Con› seil  de  securite,  dans  ses  resolutions  402  ( 1976)  du 22  decembre  1976  et  407  (1977)  du  25  mai  1977, I' Assemblee  generale,  dans  ses  resolutions  32/98  du 13 decembre  1977.  33/ 128  du  19  decembre  1978. 34/130  du  14  decembre  1979,  35/96  du  5  decembre 1980  et 36/219  du  17  decembre  1981.  et le  Secretaire general ont lances a tous  Jes  Etats, aux organisations regionales  et  intergouvernementales  et  aux  organis› mes competents des Nations Unies pour qu'ils contri› buent  genereusement  au  programme  international d'assistance afin  de  permettre  au  Lesotho de  mener a bien son developpement economique et de le  mettre mieux a meme  d'appliquer integralement  les  resolu› tions de !'Organisation des Nations Unies,
text_en: Strongly endorsing the calls for the Security Council, in its resolutions 402 (1976) of 22 December 1976 and 407 (1977) of 25 May 1977, to the General Assembly, in its resolutions 32/98 of 13 December 1977. 33/128 of 19 December 1978. 34/130 of 14 December 1979, 35/96 of 5 December 1980 and 36/219 of 17 December 1981. and the Secretary-General have launched to all States, regional and intergovernmental organizations and organizations of the United Nations to make their contribution to the international assistance programme in order to enable Lesotho to carry out its economic development successfully and to make it better able to implement fully the resolu tions of the United Nations,

text_fr: Ayant examine le  rapport  du  Secretaire  general7°. auquel  est joint  en  annexe  le  rapport  de  la  mission qu'il a  envoyee au  Lesotho, conformement a la  reso› lution  36/219  de  I' Assemblee  generale,  pour  etudier la  situation  economique  et  examiner l'etat d'avance› ment du  programme special  d'assistance economique en faveur du Lesotho,
text_en: Having examined the report of the Secretary-General7 to which the report of his mission to Lesotho is annexed, in accordance with General Assembly resolution 36/219, to examine the economic situation and to examine the state of advance of the special economic assistance programme for Lesotho,

text_fr: Notant la priorite que le  Gouvernement du  Lesotho accorde a l'augmentation de la production alimentaire. grace a une productivite accrue, afin de rendre le pays moins  tributaire  de I' Afrique  du  Sud  pour !'importa› tion de denrees alimentaires,
text_en: Noting the priority accorded by the Government of Lesotho to the increase in food production, thanks to increased productivity, in order to make the country less dependent on South Africa for the import of food products,

text_fr: Consciente du fait que le  prix  eleve que le  Lesotho doit payer pour importer des  produits  petroliers,  par suite de !'embargo sur le  petrole impose contre I' Afri› que  du  Sud,  constitue  maintenant  un  serieux  obsta› cle au  developpement du pays.
text_en: Aware of the fact that the high price Lesotho must pay to import oil products, as a result of the oil embargo, imposes on Africa that from the South, is now a serious obstacle to the country's development.

text_fr: Reconnaisrnnt, a propos  d'embargos  de  cette nature, que la communaute internationale a !'obligation d'aider les  pays  qui,  tel  le  Lesotho.  agissent  dans le  sens  de  la  Charle  des  Nations  Unies  et  en  appli› cation des resolutions de I' Assemblee generale.
text_en: Recognise, in relation to such embargoes, that the international community has an obligation to assist countries, such as Lesotho, which act in the direction of the United Nations Charle and in implementing the resolutions of the General Assembly.

text_fr: Rappeillnt ses  resolutions  32/ 160  du  19  decembre 1977 et 33/197 du 29 janvier 1979, relatives a la  Decen› nie  des transports et des communications en  Afrique, et  notant a ce  propos  que  la  situation  geopolitique du  Lesotho  exige  d'urgence  le  developpement  de liaisons  aeriennes  et  de  reseaux  de  telecommunica› tions  avec  les  pays  africains  voisins  et  ave.:  le  reste du  monde,
text_en: Repeillent its resolutions 32/160 of 19 December 1977 and 33/197 of 29 January 1979 on transport and communications in Africa, and noting in this connection that the geopolitical situation in Lesotho urgently requires the development of air links and telecommunications networks with neighbouring African countries and with the rest of the world,

text_fr: Tenant  co111pte du  fait  que  le  Lesotho  a  besoin d'un reseau  routier national.  tant  en  vue  de  mener a bien son plan de developpement social et economique que  de  se  rendre  moins  tributaire  du  reseau  sud› africain,  pour atteindre  les  diverses  regions  du  pays qui sont touchees par les restrictions qu 'impose r Afri› que du  Sud  sur les  deplacements.
text_en: Taking co111pt of the fact that Lesotho needs a national road network. both in order to carry out its social and economic development plan and to make itself less dependent on the South African network›, to reach the various regions of the country which are affected by the restrictions imposed by r Afri› and by the South on travel.

text_fr: Prenant  note des  problemes  spe.:iaux  que  connait le  Lesotho  du  fait  que  nombre  de  ses  ressortissants aptes au  travail  sont employes en Afrique du  Sud,
text_en: Taking note of the special problems that Lesotho is experiencing as many of its able-bodied nationals are employed in South Africa,

text_fr: Prl'lwnt note  l'J.?ale111ent de  la  priorite  que  le  Gou› vernement  du  Lesotho  a  accordee  au  probleme  de !'integration a l'economie de la jeune generation ainsi que  des  travailleurs  migrants  rentrant  d' Afrique  du Sud,
text_en: It notes J.?ale111ent of the priority which the Government of Lesotho has given to the problem of integration into the economy of the young generation as well as migrant workers returning from South Africa,

items_fr: Accueillant ill"l'C .rnti\ft1ctio11 Jes  mesures prises par le  Gouvernement du  Lesotho en  vue  d'integrer plus efficacement les femmes  au  processus de  developpe› ment  en  encourageant leur participation a la  vie  eco› nomique, sociale et culturelle du  pays,
items_en: Welcoming the steps taken by the Government of Lesotho to integrate women more effectively into the development process by encouraging their participation in the country's economic, social and cultural life,

text_fr: Tcnllnt  co111pte  egale111e11t du  fail  que  le  Lesotho est  non  seulement  un  pays  sans  littoral,  mais  qu'il figure  au  nombre  des  pays  Jes  moins  avances  et  les plus  gravement touches.
text_en: Tcnllnt co111pte equale111e11t of the fail that Lesotho is not only a landlocked country, but that it is one of the least advanced and most seriously affected Is countries.

text_fr: Rappelunt sa  resolution  32/98.  dans  laquelle  elle  a notamment  reconnu  que  l'afflux constant de  refugies d · Afrique  du  Sud  imposait  au  Lesotho  un  fardeau supplementaire.
text_en: It recalls its resolution 32/98, in which it recognized, inter alia, that the constant influx of refugees from South Africa placed an additional burden on Lesotho.

Re110111, which it has launched to the Member States, regional and interregio organizations and other intergovernmental organizations, to provide financial, material and technical assistance to Lesotho, in order to enable the implementation of the various projects and programmes which have not yet been financed, as defined in the report of the Secretary-General:
7. Asks Member States and relevant financial institutions, organizations and institutions to provide assistance to Lesotho to enable it to achieve greater self-sufficiency in food production:
8. D'Rally to the Member States to provide Lesotho with all possible assistance to ensure a regular supply of fuel that meets its national needs:
9. Asks Member States in 011trc to help Lesotho develop its air and road network and its air links with the rest of the world:

10. Welcomes the efforts made by the Government of Lesotho to involve women more fully in its development activities and requests the Secretary-General to consult with the Government on the type and volume of assistance needed to achieve the following objective:

I. Calls on the international community to pay attention to the meeting of donors held in Lesotho from 5 to 9 November 1979, as well as to the conference on the agricultural sector held in Lesotho from 20 to 24 October 1980, and urges Member States and appropriate institutions and agencies to provide assistance to Lesotho in accordance with the results of these meetings:

12. Appellation of the international naute to the special account opened by the Secretary-General at the United Nations Headquarters in accordance with Security Council Resolution 407 (1977) in order to facilitate the flow of contributions to Lesotho:

The United Nations Development Programme, the United Nations Children's Fund, the World Health Organization, the United Nations Industrial Development Organization, the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations and the International Fund for Agricultural Development, once again drew the attention of their governing bodies to the special needs of Lesotho and reported on the measures taken by these bodies to the Secretary-General before 15 August 1983:

14. At the request of the specialized agencies and other competent bodies of the United Nations to cooperate closely with the Secretary-General with a view to organizing an effective international programme of assistance to Lesotho and to report periodically to the Secretary-General on the measures they have taken and the resources they have made available to assist countries:
15. Pric the Secretary General:
11) Pursuing its efforts to mobilize the necessary resources has an effective programme of technical and material financial assistance in Lesotho:

(h) To hold consultations with the Government of Lesotho on the issue of migrant workers returning from South Africa and to identify the type of assistance that the Government needs to carry out labour intensive projects to reinvigorate these workers in the national economy:
(c) To ensure that the necessary financial and budgetary arrangements are made to follow up I organize the international assistance programme for Lesotho and the mobilization of assistance:

(d) To keep the situation in Lesotho under constant review. to keep in close contact with Member States. regional organizations and other intergovernmental organizations, specialized institutions and international financial institutions interested, and to report to the Economic and Social Council at its second session in 1983 on the progress of the special economic assistance programme for Lesotho:

to report on the economic developments in Lesotho and on the progress made in the organization and implementation of the special economic assistance programme for Lesotho, in time for the General Assembly to consider the matter at its thirty-eighth session.
